focus: Belgium tax-benefit surface with federal, Brussels, Flemish, Walloon, and community-layer coverage
purpose: Household-level comparison-oracle and supporting-source references for Belgium Axiom coverage. None of these are legal authority.
oracle_scope: A RuleSpec oracle must be an executable, pinned external calculator that accepts household-level inputs and returns household-level tax-benefit outputs comparable to Axiom outputs. Aggregate simulators, distributional reports, parameter documentation, and public model summaries are supporting references only, not oracles.

oracles.name: OpenFisca Belgium
oracles.role: Candidate OpenFisca comparison surface only if a real Belgium country package or API is found and pinned.

oracles.name: BELMOD
oracles.role: Potential Belgian household-level comparison reference only if a pinned executable interface can calculate the same household cases as Axiom.

oracles.name: EUROMOD Belgium country model
oracles.role: Pinned EUROMOD Belgium household-level comparison oracle for pilot output comparisons. Full BE_2025 model coverage is tracked separately because only selected output concepts are currently mapped to Axiom RuleSpec.

oracles.name: FANTASI
oracles.role: Potential detailed personal-income-tax oracle if a runnable Stata model and permitted IPCAL or synthetic input data are obtained. Public references alone are documentary, not executable oracle access.
